system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]

  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]

  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]

  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Darjiling.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Nepali.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 54
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Stock Clerks, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Urban
- state: West Bengal
- district: Darjiling
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: A 54-year-old male stock clerk, currently married, with a graduate education working in an urban West Bengal district. Nepali is his first language, and he resides in Darjiling. He is prone to health neglect and has a habit of saving cash.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

प्रक्रिया / निष्कर्ष: बिरामी 54 वर्षको पुरुष स्टक क्लर्क हुन्, जो दार्जिलिङका हुन्। उनको देब्रे पाखुरामा ठुलो, घाउ भएको गाँठो छ, जसलाई चिकित्सकीय रूपमा उच्च-ग्रेडको सफ्ट टिस्यु सार्कोमा भएको आशङ्का गरिएको छ। बिरामीले बढ्दै गरेको गाँठोको छ महिनाको इतिहास बताउँछन्, जुन सुरुमा दुखाइ नहुने तर अहिले बीच-बीचमा रगत बग्ने र हल्का असजिलोपन महसुस हुने गरेको छ। विगतको चिकित्सा इतिहासमा राम्ररी नियन्त्रित उच्च रक्तचाप र टाइप 2 मधुमेह मेलिटस समावेश छ। शल्यक्रिया पूर्वको अनुसन्धानमा देब्रे पाखुराको MRI ले 7x5 cm को विजातीय गाँठो र इन्फिल्ट्रेटिभ मार्जिन तथा वरपरको एडिमा देखाएको छ, र CT छातीले फोक्सोमा मेटास्टेसिसको कुनै प्रमाण देखाएको छैन। बिरामीलाई नेगेटिभ मार्जिनका साथ वाइड लोकल एक्साइज र सम्भावित पुनर्निर्माणको आवश्यकताका बारेमा परामर्श दिइएको थियो। प्रक्रिया सामान्य एनेस्थेसिया अन्तर्गत सम्पन्न गरियो। माथिल्लो पाखुरामा टर्निकेट लगाइयो। गाँठोको वरिपरि 5 cm को क्लिनिकल मार्जिनका साथ अण्डाकार चीरा लगाइयो। डिसेक्सन गहिरो फासियासम्म जारी रह्यो, र ट्युमरलाई ओभरल्याइङ छाला, सबकुटेनियस टिस्यु र मुनिको गहिरो फासियाका साथै एन ब्लक निकालियो। गहिरो मार्जिनको इन्ट्राअपरेटिभ फ्रोजन सेक्सन विश्लेषण ट्युमरका लागि नेगेटिभ थियो। परिणामस्वरूप बनेको दोषको नाप लगभग 12x8 cm थियो। एउटा फ्री एन्टेरोल्याटरल थाई (ALT) फ्ल्याप सङ्कलन गरियो र अपरेटिङ माइक्रोस्कोप प्रयोग गरेर रेडियल आर्टरी र भेने कोमिटेन्टेससँग माइक्रोभास्कुलर रूपमा एनास्टोमोस गरियो। फ्ल्यापलाई भित्र राखियो र सुरक्षित गरियो, र छालालाई स्टेप्लरले बन्द गरियो। टर्निकेट हटाइयो, र हेमोस्टेसिस पुष्टि गरियो। बिरामीले प्रक्रिया राम्ररी सहन गरे र शल्यक्रिया पछिको निगरानीका लागि ICU मा स्थानान्तरण गरियो।
शल्यक्रिया पछिको योजना: बिरामीलाई 24 घण्टासम्म ICU मा फ्ल्यापको नजिकबाट निगरानी गर्दै व्यवस्थापन गरिनेछ। उनले प्रोफिल्याक्टिक एन्टिबायोटिक्स, एनाल्जेसिक्स प्राप्त गर्नेछन् र उनको उच्च रक्तचाप र मधुमेहको निरन्तर व्यवस्थापन गरिनेछ। शल्यक्रिया पछिको 6 घण्टामा र त्यसपछि दैनिक रूपमा फ्ल्यापको पोस्ट-अपरेटिभ डप्लर अल्ट्रासाउन्ड गरिनेछ। बिरामीलाई क्लियर लिक्विड डाइट सुरु गरिनेछ र सहन सक्ने गरी अगाडि बढाइनेछ। शल्यक्रियाको घाउको दैनिक निरीक्षण गरिनेछ। बिरामी र उनकी श्रीमती, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Anita Rai]], लाई फ्ल्यापको समस्याका लक्षणहरूका बारेमा शिक्षित गरियो र कुनै पनि चिन्ता भएमा तुरुन्तै रिपोर्ट गर्न निर्देशन दिइयो। बिरामीको [[MRN|MRN-2024-0915-001]] अपडेट गरियो। ट्युमर बोर्डमा केसबारे छलफल गरियो, र शल्यक्रिया पछि एडजुभेन्ट रेडियोथेरापीको योजना बनाइएको छ। बिरामीको [[PATIENT_ID|PID-WB-DB-0042]] प्रणालीमा रेकर्ड गरियो।
generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|North Bengal Medical College and Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0915]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]] [[BED_NUMBER|OT-102]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Chandra Rai]] [[AGE|54]] [[GENDER|Male]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sujata Sharma]]
Procedure / findings: Patient is a 54-year-old male stock clerk from Darjiling, presenting with a large, ulcerated mass on the left forearm, clinically suspected to be a high-grade soft tissue sarcoma. The patient reports a six-month history of a growing lump, initially painless but now associated with intermittent bleeding and mild discomfort. Past medical history is significant for well-controlled hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus. Pre-operative investigations including an MRI of the forearm revealed a 7x5 cm heterogeneous mass with infiltrative margins and surrounding edema, and a CT chest showed no evidence of pulmonary metastases. The patient was counseled regarding the need for a wide local excision with negative margins and potential reconstruction. The procedure was performed under general anesthesia. A tourniquet was applied to the upper arm. An elliptical incision was made around the mass with a 5 cm clinical margin. Dissection proceeded down to the deep fascia, and the tumor was excised en bloc with the overlying skin, subcutaneous tissue, and the underlying deep fascia. Intraoperative frozen section analysis of the deep margin was negative for tumor. The resulting defect measured approximately 12x8 cm. A free anterolateral thigh (ALT) flap was harvested and microvascularly anastomosed to the radial artery and venae comitantes using the operating microscope. The flap was inset and secured, and the skin was closed with staples. The tourniquet was released, and hemostasis was confirmed. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the ICU for post-operative monitoring.
Post-operative plan: The patient will be managed in the ICU for 24 hours with close monitoring of the flap. He will receive prophylactic antibiotics, analgesics, and continued management of his hypertension and diabetes. A post-operative Doppler ultrasound of the flap will be performed in 6 hours and then daily. The patient will be started on a clear liquid diet and advanced as tolerated. The surgical wound will be inspected daily. The patient and his wife, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Anita Rai]], were educated on the signs of flap compromise and instructed to report any concerns immediately. The patient's [[MRN|MRN-2024-0915-001]] is updated. The case was discussed in the tumor board, and adjuvant radiotherapy is planned post-operatively. The patient's [[PATIENT_ID|PID-WB-DB-0042]] is recorded in the system.
judge_reasoning: Nepali Devanagari prose throughout; all TYPEs in allow-list; persona/domain fit; no script or plausibility issues; Latin IDs expected and unproblematic.

persona: Ankur Gain, a 54-year-old inventory veteran with a competitive edge, they blend meticulous stock-keeping with a love for Himalayan art, folk music, and trekking, while habitually juggling cash savings and occasional health neglect.
cultural_background: Ankur was born and raised in the bustling hill town of Darjeeling, West Bengal, where the Nepali‑speaking Gorkha community forms a core part of the social fabric. Growing up amidst tea gardens, colonial‑era architecture and a vibrant mix of Nepali, Indian and Tibetan influences, he inherited the customs of a Hindu Nepali family—celebrating festivals like Dashain and Tihar, partaking in local folk music and dance, and being familiar with the tea‑garden lifestyle. The urban setting, with its constant flow of tourists and diverse cultural events, has also exposed him to varied artistic expressions and modern ideas, feeding his curiosity for art and new experiences. His outgoing nature makes him a familiar face at community gatherings, while his competitive streak drives him to excel in his work.

Living and working in Darjeeling has given him conversational ability in Hindi, primarily for interacting with customers and colleagues, though he does not consider himself fully proficient. He has little exposure to Bengali despite residing in West Bengal, and he does not have any additional foreign language skills.

He is adept at using manual ledgers as well as computer‑based inventory software, performs accurate data entry, and maintains meticulous records. His role requires effective customer interaction, product knowledge, and basic accounting for stock reconciliation. He possesses good organizational abilities, time‑management, and team coordination skills, and he can handle high‑pressure situations in busy retail environments. His curiosity about art also equips him with a basic understanding of local handicrafts and cultural products, which helps in presenting items attractively.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Darjiling.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Nepali.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 54
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Stock Clerks, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Urban
- state: West Bengal

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|North Bengal Spinal Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Thapa]] [[AGE|42]] [[GENDER|Male]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sanjay Sharma]]
Procedure / findings: Patient presented with a traumatic fracture of the left tibia and fibula following a workplace injury at his salon. Pre-operative imaging confirmed a displaced comminuted fracture of the left tibial shaft with associated fibular fracture. The patient was taken to the operating theatre on the same day. Under general anesthesia, a left lower extremity tourniquet was applied. A longitudinal incision was made over the lateral aspect of the left leg. The fracture site was exposed, and reduction was achieved under direct visualization. The tibial shaft was stabilized with a 6mm diameter intramedullary nail. The fibular fracture was managed conservatively with casting. The wound was irrigated with saline, and layered closure was performed. Post-operative X-ray confirmed satisfactory hardware placement and alignment. The patient was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.
[[BED_NUMBER|12]]
[[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
[[RELATIVE_NAME|Sunita Thapa]]
[[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
judge_reasoning: Nepali Devanagari prose correct; all TYPEs allow-listed; male/42yo leg fracture fits domain; no script/domain/length issues.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Jalpaiguri.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Nepali.

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 42
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Hair Dressers, Barbers, Beauticians and
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: West Bengal
- district: Jalpaiguri
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: Rajkumar Sahana is a 42‑year‑old male, currently married, working as a salon owner in an urban West Bengal setting. He is Nepali‑speaking with below primary education and resides in Jalpaiguri district. His lifestyle includes occasional football play and a preference for traditional Nepali cuisine.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
ओपिडी स्लिप | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jalpaiguri District Hospital]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|JPH-2024-001]]
बिरामी: [[PATIENT_NAME|Rajkumar Sahana]] | जन्ममिति [[DOB|1982-07-22]] | उमेर: [[AGE|42]] | लिङ्ग: [[GENDER|Male]]
पेसा: [[OCCUPATION|Hair Dresser and Barber]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | डाक्टर: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Ananya Chatterjee]]
सम्बन्धित: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sunita Sahana]] | फोन: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
दर्ता कर्मचारी ID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | जिल्ला: [[DISTRICT|Jalpaiguri]]
मुख्य गुनासो: विगत दुई महिनादेखि लगातार खोकीसँगै रगतको खोकी (hemoptysis) र तौल घट्दै गएको।

वर्तमान बिरामीको इतिहास: बिरामी, 42 वर्षको पुरुष, पछिल्लो आठ हप्तादेखि सुख्खा खोकी भएको रिपोर्ट गर्दछ जुन रगतको धर्का भएको कफसँगै उत्पादक खोकीमा परिणत भयो। उनले 10 केजी तौल घटेको र कहिलेकाहीँ राती पसिना आउने कुरा पनि उल्लेख गरेका छन्। उनले ओभर-द-काउन्टर खोकी सिरपहरू प्रयोग गरे पनि कुनै राहत पाएका छैनन्।

अतीतको चिकित्सा इतिहास: बिरामीलाई पाँच वर्षअघि उच्च रक्तचापको इतिहास छ, जसको निदान गरिएको थियो र अनियमित औषधि सेवनद्वारा व्यवस्थापन गरिएको थियो। क्षयरोग वा अन्य कुनै दीर्घकालीन रोगको कुनै ज्ञात इतिहास छैन।

परीक्षण: परीक्षणमा, बिरामी क्याचेक्टिक (cachectic) अवस्थामा छन् र उनको BMI 17.5 kg/m² छ। महत्त्वपूर्ण लक्षणहरू स्थिर छन्: BP 130/85 mmHg, पल्स 88/min, श्वासप्रश्वास दर 18/min, तापक्रम 37.1°C। छातीको परीक्षणले दायाँ माथिल्लो क्षेत्रमा श्वासप्रश्वासको आवाज कम भएको र कहिलेकाहीँ ह्विज (wheezes) भएको देखाउँछ। कुनै क्लबिंग (clubbing) देखिएन।

अनुसन्धान: छातीको X-ray ले क्याभिटेसन (cavitation) सहितको दायाँ माथिल्लो लोब मास (mass) देखाउँछ। त्यसपछिको CT स्क्यानले दायाँ माथिल्लो लोबमा 4.5 cm को स्पिकुलेटेड घाउ (spiculated lesion) र मेडियास्टिनल लिम्फ्याडेनोप्याथी (mediastinal lymphadenopathy) भएको पुष्टि गर्दछ। AFB का लागि कफको परीक्षण नकारात्मक छ। फोक्सोको घाउको CT-निर्देशित बायोप्सी योजना गरिएको छ।

अस्थायी निदान: प्राथमिक फोक्सोको कार्सिनोमा, दायाँ माथिल्लो लोब, शंकास्पद चरण IIIA।

योजना: 1. अस्थायी निदान र थप अनुसन्धानको आवश्यकताका बारेमा बिरामी र उनको सम्बन्धित व्यक्तिलाई परामर्श दिने। 2. हिस्टोप्याथोलोजिकल पुष्टि (histopathological confirmation) का लागि CT-निर्देशित परक्युटेनियस फोक्सोको बायोप्सी तालिका बनाउने। 3. आधारभूत रक्त परीक्षणको व्यवस्था गर्ने जसमा CBC, कलेजो र मिर्गौलाको कार्य परीक्षण, र कोगुलेसन प्रोफाइल समावेश छ। 4. बायोप्सी पछि थप व्यवस्थापनका लागि ओन्कोलोजी विभागमा पठाउने। 5. नियमित फलो-अप र उच्च रक्तचापको औषधिको पालनाका बारेमा सल्लाह दिने।

Chief complaint: Persistent cough with hemoptysis and progressive weight loss for the past two months.

History of present illness: The patient, a 42-year-old male, reports a dry cough that progressed to productive with blood-streaked sputum over the last eight weeks. He also notes a 10 kg weight loss and occasional night sweats. He has been using over-the-counter cough syrups without relief.

Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, diagnosed five years ago and managed with irregular medication. No known history of tuberculosis or other chronic illnesses.

Examination: On examination, the patient is cachectic with a BMI of 17.5 kg/m². Vital signs are stable: BP 130/85 mmHg, pulse 88/min, respiratory rate 18/min, temperature 37.1°C. Chest examination reveals decreased breath sounds in the right upper zone with occasional wheezes. No clubbing noted.

Investigations: A chest X-ray shows a right upper lobe mass with cavitation. A subsequent CT scan confirms a 4.5 cm spiculated lesion in the right upper lobe with mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Sputum for AFB is negative. A CT-guided biopsy of the lung lesion is planned.

Provisional diagnosis: Primary lung carcinoma, right upper lobe, suspected stage IIIA.

Plan: 1. Counsel the patient and his relative regarding the provisional diagnosis and the need for further investigations. 2. Schedule a CT-guided percutaneous lung biopsy for histopathological confirmation. 3. Arrange for baseline blood work including CBC, liver and kidney function tests, and coagulation profile. 4. Refer to the oncology department for further management post-biopsy. 5. Advise on regular follow-up and adherence to medication for hypertension.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]

  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]

  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Lab Report for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Jalpaiguri.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Nepali.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 42
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Hair Dressers, Barbers, Beauticians and
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: West Bengal
- district: Jalpaiguri
- first_language: Nepali

Document format guide for Lab Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):

NEVER write [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] / [[এম.আর.এন|…]] — only PATIENT_NAME, MRN, etc.

MANDATORY inline entities (9 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:

- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]

- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Odia (code=or, script=Odia). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Psychiatry / Behavioral Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Odisha, district=Nabarangapur.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Odia for Odia.

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male

He is married, speaks Odia as his first language, and resides in a rural setting. His lifestyle includes managing workshop paperwork and engaging in community activities.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]

- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Nabarangapur District Hospital]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|NDH-2024-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Kishor Maharana]] | DOB [[DOB|1989-07-22]] | Age: [[AGE|35]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Male]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Shoe Maker and Related Workers, Other]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. Patnaik]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sushila Maharana]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9438901234]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4021]] | District: [[DISTRICT|Nabarangapur]]
Chief complaint: ରୋଗୀ ତିନି ମାସ ଧରି ଲଗାତାର କମ୍ ମନୋଭାବ, ଆନନ୍ଦହୀନତା ଏବଂ ଶୋଇବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା ବିଷୟରେ କହୁଛନ୍ତି। ସେ କାମ ଏବଂ ଘରେ ଅଧିକ ବିରକ୍ତି ଅନୁଭବ କରୁଥିବା ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି।

History of present illness: ଶ୍ରୀ କିଶୋର ମହାରଣା, ଜଣେ 35 ବର୍ଷର ପୁରୁଷ ମୋଚି, ଗତ ତିନି ମାସ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଅବସାଦ ଲକ୍ଷଣର ଧୀରେ ଧୀରେ ଆରମ୍ଭ ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ଏକ ବ୍ୟାପକ କମ୍ ମନୋଭାବ, ନିଜର ସାଧାରଣ କାମ ଏବଂ ସାମାଜିକ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକଳାପରେ ଆଗ୍ରହ ହରାଇବା ଏବଂ ନିଜକୁ ଅଦରକାରୀ ମନେ କରିବା ବିଷୟରେ ବର୍ଣ୍ଣନା କରିଛନ୍ତି। ଶୋଇବା ବାଧାପ୍ରାପ୍ତ ହୋଇଛି, ଶୀଘ୍ର ଶୋଇବା ଉଠିବା ଏବଂ ପୁଣି ଶୋଇବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା ହେଉଛି। ଭୋକ କମିଯାଇଛି ଏବଂ ସେ ପ୍ରାୟ 5 କିଲୋଗ୍ରାମ ଓଜନ ହରାଇଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ଅଧିକ ବିରକ୍ତି ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି, ଯାହା ଫଳରେ ତାଙ୍କ ସ୍ତ୍ରୀଙ୍କ ସହ ଯୁକ୍ତିତର୍କ ହେଉଛି ଏବଂ ତାଙ୍କ କର୍ମଶାଳାର ହିସାବ ରଖିବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା ହେଉଛି। କୌଣସି ମାନସିକ ଲକ୍ଷଣ, ଆତ୍ମହତ୍ୟା ଚିନ୍ତା କିମ୍ବା ନିଜକୁ କ୍ଷତି ପହଞ୍ଚାଇବା ବିଷୟରେ ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରାଯାଇ ନାହିଁ।

Past psychiatric history: ପୂର୍ବରୁ କୌଣସି ମାନସିକ ରୋଗର ନିଦାନ କିମ୍ବା ଚିକିତ୍ସା ହୋଇନାହିଁ।

Past medical history: କୌଣସି ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଚିକିତ୍ସା ରୋଗ ନାହିଁ।

Medications: କିଛି ନାହିଁ।

Family history: ମା'ଙ୍କ ବିଷୟରେ କୁହାଯାଇଛି ଯେ ତାଙ୍କର ଉଚ୍ଚ ରକ୍ତଚାପ ଅଛି। ମାନସିକ ରୋଗର କୌଣସି ଜଣାଶୁଣା ପାରିବାରିକ ଇତିହାସ ନାହିଁ।

Social history: ବିବାହିତ, ନିଜ ସ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଏବଂ ଦୁଇ ସନ୍ତାନଙ୍କ ସହ ଏକ ଗ୍ରାମାଞ୍ଚଳରେ ରହୁଛନ୍ତି। ଜଣେ ମୋଚି ଭାବରେ କାମ କରନ୍ତି, ଏକ ଛୋଟ କର୍ମଶାଳା ପରିଚାଳନା କରନ୍ତି। ତାଙ୍କ ସ୍ତ୍ରୀଙ୍କଠାରୁ ଭଲ ସାମାଜିକ ସମର୍ଥନ ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି କିନ୍ତୁ ତାଙ୍କ ମନୋଭାବ ଯୋଗୁଁ ସାମ୍ପ୍ରତିକ ଚାପ ବିଷୟରେ ଉଲ୍ଲେଖ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ତମାଖୁ କିମ୍ବା ମଦ୍ୟପାନ ବ୍ୟବହାରକୁ ମନା କରିଛନ୍ତି।

Examination:
Mental state examination: ରୋଗୀ ଭଲ ପୋଷାକ ପିନ୍ଧିଛନ୍ତି କିନ୍ତୁ କ୍ଳାନ୍ତ ଦେଖାଯାଉଛନ୍ତି। କଥାବାର୍ତ୍ତା ସ୍ୱାଭାବିକ ଗତିରେ ଏବଂ ପରିମାଣରେ ହେଉଛି, ବେଳେବେଳେ ବିରତି ସହିତ। ମନୋଭାବ "କମ୍" ଏବଂ "ଅବସାଦଗ୍ରସ୍ତ" ଭାବରେ ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରାଯାଇଛି। ଭାବନା ସୀମିତ ଅଛି। ଚିନ୍ତା ପ୍ରକ୍ରିୟା ଯୁକ୍ତିଯୁକ୍ତ ଏବଂ ସୁସଙ୍ଗତ। କୌଣସି ଭ୍ରମ କିମ୍ବା ହାଲୁସିନେସନ୍ ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରାଯାଇ ନାହିଁ। ଅନ୍ତର୍ଦୃଷ୍ଟି ଠିକ୍ ଅଛି, ନିଜ ଅବସ୍ଥାର ଗୁରୁତରତାକୁ ଗ୍ରହଣ କରିବାରେ କିଛି ଅସୁବିଧା ହେଉଛି। ବିରକ୍ତି ସମ୍ବନ୍ଧରେ ବିଚାର ଶକ୍ତି ବାଧାପ୍ରାପ୍ତ ହୋଇଛି।

Physical examination: ସ୍ୱାଭାବିକ ସୀମା ମଧ୍ୟରେ।

Diagnosis:
1. Major Depressive Disorder, Single Episode, Moderate (F32.1)
2. Insomnia (G47.00)

Treatment plan:
1. Sertraline 50 mg ଦିନକୁ ଥରେ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରନ୍ତୁ, ଏକ ସପ୍ତାହ ପରେ ସହନଶୀଳତା ଅନୁଯାୟୀ 100 mg କୁ ବୃଦ୍ଧି କରନ୍ତୁ।
2. ଅବସାଦ, ଏହାର ଚିକିତ୍ସା ଏବଂ ଅନୁପାଳନର ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱ ବିଷୟରେ ମନୋ-ଶିକ୍ଷା ପ୍ରଦାନ କରନ୍ତୁ।
3. ଶୋଇବା ସ୍ୱଚ୍ଛତା ଅଭ୍ୟାସ ବିଷୟରେ ପରାମର୍ଶ ଦିଅନ୍ତୁ।
4. ଚିକିତ୍ସାର ପ୍ରତିକ୍ରିୟା ଏବଂ ପାର୍ଶ୍ୱ ପ୍ରତିକ୍ରିୟା ଆକଳନ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ଦୁଇ ସପ୍ତାହ ପରେ ଏକ ଫଲୋ-ଅପ୍ ଆପଏଣ୍ଟମେଣ୍ଟ୍ ସ୍ଥିର କରନ୍ତୁ।
5. ସହାୟକ ମନୋଚିକିତ୍ସା ପାଇଁ କାଉନସେଲିଂ ସେଣ୍ଟରକୁ ପଠାନ୍ତୁ।

generated_text_en:
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Nabarangapur District Hospital]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|NDH-2024-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Kishor Maharana]] | DOB [[DOB|1989-07-22]] | Age: [[AGE|35]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Male]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Shoe Maker and Related Workers, Other]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. Patnaik]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sushila Maharana]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9438901234]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4021]] | District: [[DISTRICT|Nabarangapur]]
Chief complaint: Patient presents with persistent low mood, anhedonia, and difficulty sleeping for the past three months, reporting increased irritability at work and home.

History of present illness: Mr. Kishor Maharana, a 35-year-old male shoemaker, reports a gradual onset of depressive symptoms over the last three months. He describes a pervasive low mood, loss of interest in his usual craft and community activities, and feelings of worthlessness. Sleep is disturbed with early morning awakening and difficulty returning to sleep. Appetite is reduced, and he has lost approximately 5 kg in weight. He reports increased irritability, leading to arguments with his spouse, and difficulty concentrating on his workshop accounts. There are no reported psychotic symptoms, suicidal ideation, or self-harm.

Past psychiatric history: No prior psychiatric diagnoses or treatment.

Past medical history: No significant medical illnesses.

Medications: None.

Family history: Mother is reportedly hypertensive. No known family history of psychiatric illness.

Social history: Married, lives in a rural village with his wife and two children. Works as a shoemaker, managing a small workshop. Reports good social support from his wife but notes recent strain due to his mood. Denies tobacco or alcohol use.

Examination:
Mental state examination: Patient is well-dressed but appears fatigued. Speech is of normal rate and volume, with occasional pauses. Mood is reported as "low" and "depressed." Affect is constricted. Thought process is logical and coherent. No delusions or hallucinations reported. Insight is fair, with some difficulty in accepting the severity of his condition. Judgment is impaired regarding his irritability.

Physical examination: Within normal limits.

Diagnosis:
1. Major Depressive Disorder, Single Episode, Moderate (F32.1)
2. Insomnia (G47.00)

Treatment plan:
1. Initiate antidepressant therapy with Sertraline 50 mg once daily, to be increased to 100 mg after one week as tolerated.
2. Provide psychoeducation regarding depression, its treatment, and the importance of adherence.
3. Advise on sleep hygiene practices.
4. Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess response to treatment and side effects.
5. Refer to the counseling center for supportive psychotherapy.

cultural_background: Born and raised in the rural village of Nabarangapur, Odisha, Kishor Maharana hails from a traditional Odia artisan community where hand-crafted footwear has been passed down through generations. His cultural life revolves around village festivals like Rath Yatra, Nuakhai and local worship of village deities, as well as communal storytelling and folk music. While deeply rooted in these customs, his innate curiosity leads him to experiment with modest design innovations, reflecting a practical blend of tradition and modernity.
linguistic_background: Kishor’s first language is Odia, which he speaks, reads and writes with confidence, allowing him to manage day‑to‑day transactions, follow local news and keep records for his workshop. He has a basic functional understanding of Hindi, mainly for official communication and occasional market dealings, but he does not read or write Hindi fluently. His schooling only went up to primary level, so his exposure to English is minimal, limited to a few borrowed terms used in the trade.
skills_and_expertise: Kishor is skilled in hand‑crafted shoe making, including leather cutting, pattern drafting, hand stitching, and finishing. He can design simple styles that suit local preferences and repair worn footwear efficiently. His practical mindset ensures consistent quality, while his curiosity pushes him to experiment with modest design tweaks. He also manages the day‑to‑day operations of his small workshop, handling inventory, basic bookkeeping, and direct customer service with a cooperative and reliable approach.

hobbies_and_interests: In his free time, Kishor enjoys listening to Odia folk songs and bhajans, occasionally playing the dholak during village gatherings. He follows cricket matches on the radio and likes to watch short tutorial videos on shoe design on his mobile phone. Gardening, especially growing seasonal vegetables, offers him quiet relaxation, while cooking traditional Odia dishes such as dalma and roti lets him share meals with family and neighbours. He plans to learn basic modern tailoring tools, improve design variety, and train a couple of apprentices from his village.
